defs: That which makes evident or manifest; that which furnishes, or tends to furnish, proof; any mode of proof; the ground of belief or judgement; as, the evidence of our senses; evidence of the truth or falsehood of a statement.
quotes: Faith is . . . the evidence of things not seen.
O glorious trial of exceeding love Illustrious evidence, example high.

defs: That which is legally submitted to competent tribunal, as a means of ascertaining the truth of any alleged matter of fact under investigation before it; means of making proof; -- the latter, strictly speaking, not being synonymous with evidence, but rather the effect of it.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Evidenced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Evidencing (?).]

defs: To render evident or clear; to prove; to evince; as, to evidence a fact, or the guilt of an offender.
